channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Musk and Trump are viewed roughly the same by Americans, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk, clad in tuxedo and black tie, took the stage at President-elect Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort shortly after the election with all the swagger of the winning candidate himself. “The public has given us a mandate that could not be more clear, the clearest mandate.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, SARAH PARVINI and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why AI company Databricks just scored one of the biggest funding rounds in history
item.description: The San Francisco-based data analytics company Databricks has raised a stunning $10 billion funding round, which values the company at $62 billion and likely puts it on a path toward an IPO.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: This new free AI chatbot protects Discord users with AI technology to identify potential scams
item.description: Bitdefender's Scamio is a free AI-powered tool designed to help users identify and combat scams online.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: EPA fosters IT resilience through cloud, integrated teams, automation tools
item.description: EPA's CIO says modernized tools and integrated teams help the agency meet the changing needs of the public more easily and consistently. The post EPA fosters IT resilience through cloud, integrated teams, automation tools first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok asks the Supreme Court to block the U.S. ban
item.description: TikTok on Monday asked the Supreme Court to step in on an emergency basis to block the federal law that would ban the popular platform in the United States unless its China-based parent company agreed to sell it.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Government Agencies Required to Address Cloud Security Gaps
item.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency has issued a binding operational directive requiring federal civilian departments and agencies to evaluate and fix security gaps in their cloud computing applications.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Almost a million ConnectOnCall users may have had data stolen by hackers
item.description: 900,000 patients who called doctors out of hours may have had their information stolen following data breach.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cisco grabs SnapAttack for threat detection
item.description: Cisco is acquiring threat-detection startup SnapAttack for an undisclosed amount as it continues to expand its security portfolio.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 6 ways AI can help UX designers work better—and faster
item.description: Artificial intelligence isn’t creative in the same way that people are. It doesn’t understand inspiration. But that doesn’t mean real UX designers can’t use it. AI can help a designer be more efficient and creative, and do a few more things that might be surprising.
item.dc:creator: Richard Baguley, Fast Company

item.title: 19 percent in new survey have invested in, traded or used cryptocurrency
item.description: About 1-in-5 voters have invested in, traded or used cryptocurrency, according to a Tuesday poll. The survey from Emerson College Polling found that 19 percent of respondents have traded, invested or used crypto like Bitcoin or Ethereum. About 81 percent have not.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra: A Top-Tier Smartphone with Upgraded Features
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra is expected to have improved features, including a new camera system, extra RAM for AI, and a top-end chipset. Find out what else to expect from this smartphone.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: It’s the end of the internet as we know it—and I feel fine
item.description: The internet feels like it’s falling apart. Not literally. Structurally, it’s sound. There are plenty of fiber optic cables lining ocean floors, cell towers looming above cityscapes, and server-filled data centers.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Nima Momeni found guilty in Cash App CEO's death
item.description: Nima Momeni testified he was defending himself when Cash App CEO Bob Lee was stabbed and later died at a local hospital
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: House task force releases sweeping end-of-year report on AI
item.description: The House Task Force on Artificial Intelligence (AI) released its sweeping end-of-year report Tuesday, laying out a roadmap for Congress as it crafts policy surrounding the advancing technology. The 253-page report takes a deep dive into how the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Google Agrees to Advanced Community Benefits Payment in San Jose
item.description: Google commits $5 million to community benefits tied to stalled Downtown West project, with overall package totaling $200 million.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Uh oh... Zotac just leaked Nvidia’s next-gen launch line-up, including RTX 5090 GPU with 32GB of VRAM
item.description: Zotac accidentally spills the details on Nvidia’s initial RTX 5000 range – but there’s no RTX 5060 to be seen.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: EU investigates TikTok over Romanian presidential election safeguards
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators said Tuesday they're investigating whether TikTok breached the bloc's digital rulebook by failing to deal with risks to Romania's presidential election, which has been thrown into turmoil over allegations of electoral violations and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The point-and-shoot is back: Panasonic launches new Lumix out of the blue, with iPhone-trouncing 30x optical zoom
item.description: The new Panasonic Lumix ZS99 / TZ99 point-and-shoot updates the ZS80 / TZ80 with USB-C charging and will go on sale in February 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: US moves to tighten restrictions on China Telecom amid security fears
item.description: The US Commerce Department is intensifying its actions against China Telecom’s US unit over concerns that its cloud and internet services could be used to funnel American data to Beijing, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DoD edges toward goal of ubiquitous connectivity — connect anything, provide access anywhere
item.description: DoD’s embrace of commercial technologies offers big opportunities but creates new network design and security considerations, Verizon’s Ken Gonzales explains. The post DoD edges toward goal of ubiquitous connectivity — connect anything, provide access anywhere first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: How to contact the Rhode Island cyberattack call center after data breach
item.description: Rhode Island officials urged residents to take immediate steps to protect themselves on Saturday, warning that the hackers could release personal data.
item.dc:creator: Leah Crowley, NewsNation

item.title: Founder mode can’t work. Be maximalist
item.description: The tech industry was recently abuzz with a post from Y Combinator founder Paul Graham on “founder mode.
item.dc:creator: Bipul Sinha, Fast Company

item.title: This Mark Cuban-backed startup is helping creators monetize their audiences
item.description: Falon Fatemi first met Mark Cuban in 2011 at South By Southwest, where the two bonded over ideas about untapped opportunities in the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: Janya Sundar, Fast Company

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Harris campaign aide accuses Musk of throttling content before election
item.description: A campaign aide to Vice President Harris accused tech billionaire Elon Musk of throttling content on his social platform X ahead of the 2024 election to benefit President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: How to upgrade to the Hyte Y70 PC case to give your parts more breathing room
item.description: The Hyte Y70 is a stylish PC case that will help PC gamers get the most out of their graphics cards and other high-end parts
item.dc:creator: Gieson Cacho, The Mercury News

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: From surveillance to safety: How Kazakhstan’s Carpet CCTV is reshaping security
item.description: In a world where technology increasingly shapes how cities manage safety and security, Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs is leading the way with its groundbreaking “Carpet CCTV” project.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Azure hardware innovations and the serverless cloud future
item.description: Microsoft uses its events to lift the hood on Azure’s hardware and the infrastructure it uses to manage and run its services. Ignite 2024 was no different.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google claims breakthrough with Willow quantum computing chip but no real-world use yet
item.description: Google’s announcement on Monday of its new quantum chip, called Willow, was full of eye-catching statements. For starters, Willow took less than a minute to perform a benchmark computation that would take one of today’s fastest supercomputers 10 septillion years to do.
item.dc:creator: Network World
